steps.title: Find the mass of one object
steps.content:
If $r$ identical objects have total mass $t$, then divide the total mass by the number of objects to find the mass of each object:
$$\frac{t}{r}$$

steps.title: Scale to the requested count
steps.content:
The question asks for the total mass of $132r$ identical objects, so multiply the mass of one object by $132r$:
$$132r\cdot\frac{t}{r}=132t$$
The factor $r$ cancels because the number of objects is $132$ times the original number. The expression is $132t$. The answer is D.
